2606.30161.title: Federated Learning with Energy-Based Structured Probabilistic Inference
2606.30161.paper_summary: Federated learning typically aggregates client updates using fixed or heuristic weighting rules, which can be suboptimal when clients have heterogeneous data and varying contributions to the global model. We propose a framework that refines client aggregation weights using Conditional Random Fields (CRFs). Our method defines unary potentials for individual clients and pairwise potentials for all client pairs, allowing the server to model both client-specific reliability and interactions between clients. The resulting CRF inference produces aggregation weights that enable better convergence of the global training objective. Experiments show that, under non-IID heterogeneity, our approach consistently improves performance over well-established federated learning baselines.
2606.30161.paper_summary_zh: 聯邦學習通常使用固定或啟發式的加權規則來聚合客戶端更新，當客戶端擁有異質數據和對全球模型的貢獻不同時，這可能不是最佳選擇。<lb/>我們提出了一個框架，通過條件隨機場（CRFs）來細化客戶端的聚合權重。<lb/>我們的方法為單個客戶端定義了一元潛力，並為所有客戶端對定義了成對潛力，這使得伺服器能夠建模客戶端特定的可靠性和客戶端之間的互動。<lb/>最終的CRF推斷產生的聚合權重能夠促進全球訓練目標的更好收斂。<lb/>實驗表明，在非獨立同分佈的異質性下，我們的方法在性能上始終優於成熟的聯邦學習基準。
2606.30161.author: Dario Fenoglio et.al.
2606.30161.authors: Dario Fenoglio, Daniil Kirilenko, Martin Gjoreski, Marc Langheinrich
